Der Ansatz erreicht hochwertige Ergebnisse bei mehreren Korruptionstypen und bewahrt gleichzeitig einen minimalen Rechenaufwand.

es.title: Estabilización de Características de Vista de Pájaro Después de la Fusión para Detección 3D Multimodal Robusta
es.summary: El documento introduce un módulo post-fusión ligero que estabiliza características de vista de pájaro en detectores de fusión cámara-lidar, mejorando la robustez bajo cambio de dominio y fallas de sensores. El enfoque logra resultados de última generación en múltiples tipos de corrupción mientras mantiene una huella computacional mínima.

title: Relational Semantic Reasoning on 3D Scene Graphs for Open World Interactive Object Search
summary: SCOUT enables robots to search for objects in household environments by reasoning about semantic relationships in 3D scene graphs, using lightweight models distilled from large language models. The method achieves computational efficiency comparable to embedding-based approaches while matching large language model performance.
content:
arXiv:2603.05642v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Open-world interactive object search in household environments requires understanding semantic relationships between objects and their surrounding context to guide exploration efficiently. Prior methods either rely on vision-language embeddings similarity, which does not reliably capture task-relevant relational semantics, or large language models (LLMs), which are too slow and costly for real-time deployment. We introduce SCOUT: Scene Graph-Based Exploration with Learned Utility for Open-World Interactive Object Search, a novel method that searches directly over 3D scene graphs by assigning utility scores to rooms, frontiers, and objects using relational exploration heuristics such as room-object containment and object-object co-occurrence. To make this practical without sacrificing open-vocabulary generalization, we propose an offline procedural distillation framework that extracts structured relational knowledge from LLMs into lightweight models for on-robot inference. Furthermore, we present SymSearch, a scalable symbolic benchmark for evaluating semantic reasoning in interactive object search tasks. Extensive evaluations across symbolic and simulation environments show that SCOUT outperforms embedding similarity-based methods and matches LLM-level performance while remaining computationally efficient. Finally, real-world experiments demonstrate effective transfer to physical environments, enabling open-world interactive object search under realistic sensing and navigation constraints.

zh.title: 用于开放世界交互式对象搜索的3D场景图关系语义推理
zh.summary: SCOUT通过推理3D场景图中的语义关系，使机器人能够在家庭环境中搜索对象，使用从大型语言模型中提取的轻量级模型。该方法在计算效率上与基于嵌入的方法相当，同时与大型语言模型的性能相匹配。

fr.title: Raisonnement Sémantique Relationnel sur les Graphes de Scènes 3D pour la Recherche d'Objets Interactifs en Monde Ouvert
fr.summary: SCOUT permet aux robots de rechercher des objets dans des environnements domestiques en raisonnant sur les relations sémantiques dans les graphes de scènes 3D, en utilisant des modèles légers distillés à partir de grands modèles de langage. La méthode atteint une efficacité informatique comparable aux approches basées sur les plongements tout en correspondant aux performances des grands modèles de langage.

de.title: Relationales semantisches Reasoning auf 3D-Szenengraphen für die offene interaktive Objektsuche
de.summary: SCOUT ermöglicht es Robotern, Objekte in Haushaltsumgebungen zu suchen, indem sie semantische Beziehungen in 3D-Szenengraphen analysieren und leichte Modelle verwenden, die aus großen Sprachmodellen destilliert wurden. Die Methode erreicht Recheneffizienz, die mit einbettungsgestützten Ansätzen vergleichbar ist, und entspricht gleichzeitig der Leistung großer Sprachmodelle.

es.title: Razonamiento Semántico Relacional en Gráficos de Escenas 3D para Búsqueda de Objetos Interactivos en Mundo Abierto
es.summary: SCOUT permite a los robots buscar objetos en entornos domésticos razonando sobre relaciones semánticas en gráficos de escenas 3D, utilizando modelos ligeros destilados de grandes modelos de lenguaje. El método logra eficiencia computacional comparable a los enfoques basados en incrustaciones mientras se adapta al rendimiento de los grandes modelos de lenguaje.
